title: The Shipment
stakes: A sealed data-case, a sector someone is quietly closing, and a fixer whose cut prices in your funeral.

world.name: Terra
world.flavor: a neon-drowned sprawl where the corps own the sky and everything below it is for sale

start_location.name: The Sprawl Fringe
start_location.description: The edge of the night market, where the neon thins into the dark between towers and the crowd loosens. Rain sheets off the awnings; a noodle stall's steam catches the signage glow.

second_location.name: Terra Night Market
second_location.description: A fixer's booth deep in Terra's night market — stalls of counterfeit chrome, leaked data, and street docs working under flickering neon. Vesa's table sits in the back, a sealed data-case still warm between you.

third_location.name: The Watched Sector
third_location.description: The sector line the case has to cross — checkpoint drones, corp cordons, and a ripperdoc's back-alley clinic where couriers get patched or don't come back.

opening.situation: A fixer's booth in the Terra night market. A sealed data-case sits on the table between you, still warm. The job is simple until it isn't: carry it across a sector someone is quietly strangling shut.

dialogueBeats.label: The job
dialogueBeats.text: "Simple run. The case crosses the sector, you don't open it, you don't stop. My cut's steep — but so's the funeral if you botch it."

dialogueBeats.label: The last courier
dialogueBeats.text: "I patched the last one who carried a case like that. What was left of them. They said the collector doesn't stop."

offer_courier.title: Run the case
offer_courier.summary: Carry the sealed data-case across the sector to the drop, unopened.

quest_courier.title: Deliver the case
quest_courier.summary: Get the sealed data-case across the watched sector to the drop.

quest_netrun.title: The one-try vault
quest_netrun.summary: A corp black-ICE site under the dead substation. One jack-in, no second try.

fronts.title: The Run
fronts.agenda: The job is live; the client wants the case moved tonight, and the finish line is not where it was.

beats.label: The run is live
beats.telegraph: Vesa slides the case an inch closer and takes her hand off it.
beats.brief: The courier contract is live — the case is yours to move, unopened, across the sector.
beats.decision: Take the case and move, or press Vesa for what's really inside.

fact.text: The courier contract is live: the sealed case moves tonight, and the client is watching the clock.

beats.label: The drop shifts
beats.brief: The drop point shifted — the client is spooked and the finish line just moved.
beats.decision: Chase the new drop, or hold and demand to know why it moved.

fact.text: The drop point shifted without warning — the client is spooked, and the case is hotter than the job priced.

fronts.title: The One-Try Vault
fronts.agenda: A corp black-ICE site under the dead substation; probing it is what makes the owning corp notice you.

beats.label: Runners talk
beats.telegraph: Two fixers go quiet when you pass, then start again lower.
beats.brief: Runners talk of a vault under the dead substation — one jack-in, no second try.
beats.decision: File the rumor and move on, or start asking who's been down there.

fact.text: Runners talk of a vault under the dead substation — one jack-in, no second try, and no one who tried it twice.

beats.label: The one-try test
beats.telegraph: A maintenance hatch you passed twice is now ajar.
beats.brief: The way into the substation vault is open — and the corp logs every jack-in.
beats.decision: Jack in for the one attempt, or walk away clean.

fronts.title: The Collector's Cut
fronts.agenda: The one closing the sector wants the case — and to make an example of the courier.

beats.label: Your gig got flagged
beats.telegraph: A cheap drone tags your route and peels off toward the grid towers.
beats.brief: Someone with pull on the sector grid knows a courier took the case — the moment it changed hands.
beats.decision: Push on fast before word travels, or go dark and reroute.

fact.text: The sector grid flagged the courier the moment the case changed hands — someone is already watching the routes.

beats.label: The cordon hardens
beats.telegraph: Checkpoint lights double along the only clean route out.
beats.brief: The sector cordon tightens; the clean route is now watched and slow.
beats.decision: Bribe through, find a gap, or risk the run and lose time you don't have.

objectState.reason: The sector cordon is manned and scanning; passage costs more now.

beats.label: The collector arrives
beats.telegraph: A gonk in corp leathers is asking your face by name.
beats.brief: A collector sent by the one closing the sector has found the courier — he wants the case, and he has not come to bargain long.
beats.decision: Hand it over, talk him down, or refuse and face him.

dialogueBeats.label: Hand it over
dialogueBeats.text: "The reeve says the sector's his now — and so's what moves on it. The case. Or we take it off what's left of you."

beats.label: The reeve has a name
beats.telegraph: The collector's dead comm still pings one handle, over and over.
beats.brief: The collector answered to someone with a name — and that someone now knows the courier's face.
beats.decision: Hunt the reeve before they send another, or run the case and stay ahead of them.

fact.text: The collector answered to Kesh, who runs the sector tolls — and now Kesh knows the courier's face and that the courier fights.

secrets.text: The case isn't cargo. It's someone's engram backup — that's why the tax, and why the collector won't stop.
Vesa's cut is triple the going rate. She's not being generous — she's pricing in the funeral.
The substation 'lock' is a neural fuse, not ICE. 'One try' means one brain.
